item.title: Joint House hearings on Middle East crisis response to begin April 8 — Quimbo
item.description: Joint hearings in the House of Representatives aimed at establishing a legislative response to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East or West Asia will begin on April 8, House Committee on Ways and Means Chairperson Miro Quimbo said Tuesday.

item.title: Batangas Rep. Leviste fails to secure a copy of ICI final report
item.description: On the last day of operation of the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) on Tuesday, March 31, Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Leviste tried to secure a copy of the final report of the commission tasked to investigate anomalous flood control projects.

item.title: 'Not an escalation': NSC says China activities in West PH Sea part of 'standard operations'
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard confirmed that Chinese naval and air patrols were monitored over Bajo de Masinloc or Scarborough Shoal over the weekend, shortly after the Bilateral Consultative Mechanism between Manila and Beijing was conducted in Fujian, China.

item.title: Visa relief for stranded foreigners in PH due to Middle East war extended to May 1
item.description: The Department of Justice extended the visa relief for foreign nationals in the Philippines who are affected by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Vida: DOJ investigations not sidetracked by uncertainties due to Middle East tension
item.description: Justice Secretary Fredderick Vida said investigations on allegedly anomalous flood control projects are not sidetracked by uncertainties such as increasing prices of commodities due to ongoing tension in the Middle East.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
